summary: Finished matching the remaining Child Link fighter code by deleting asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s and replacing it with C in src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink_2.c, while updating build lists and function prototypes. The PR specifically notes that func_80149354 had to live in a separate file to prevent func_80149268 from being inlined, making file partitioning part of the matching strategy.
what_changed: Deleted the remaining ftclink assembly file asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s, removing asm implementations of func_80149354, func_8014943C, func_8014945C, and func_8014947C.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink_2.c with C implementations for func_80149354 and three simple wrapper functions calling func_800DED30, func_80084F3C, and func_80084280.

Moved the repeated x2244 cleanup logic into an inline helper checkFighter2244 in src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.h, then changed func_80149268 in ftclink.c to call that helper instead of containing the full logic inline.
Updated obj_files.mk to replace the ftclink.s object with src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink_2.c.o.
Added ftclink_2.c.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.

Corrected or strengthened declarations in include/functions.h: func_8007500C now takes struct _Fighter* instead of struct _Fighter, and func_802C8B28 is declared as returning void* with parameters struct _HSD_GObj*, Vec*, u8, f32.
smart_moves: Split func_80149354 into a separate translation unit, ftclink_2.c, explicitly to prevent the compiler from inlining func_80149268; this was the key matching tactic called out in the PR body.
Factored x2244 cleanup into an inline header helper so both func_80149268 and func_80149354 could share the same emitted cleanup behavior without duplicating source logic in the original file.
Preserved suspicious-looking control-flow artifacts needed for matching, such as the final redundant gobj NULL check pattern inside checkFighter2244.
Kept tiny asm wrapper functions as minimal C wrappers, reducing risk: func_8014943C delegates to func_800DED30, func_8014945C to func_80084F3C, and func_8014947C to func_80084280.
Updated prototypes before using typed calls in the newly decompiled C, avoiding reliance on unknown or commented-out declarations.
decomp_lessons: Translation-unit boundaries can be a matching tool: moving a function to a separate .c file can prevent unwanted inlining and preserve the target call shape.
Inline helpers in headers can reproduce shared instruction sequences while still allowing a caller in another translation unit to emit the desired code.
When matching old MWCC output, semantically redundant checks or no-op comparisons may be intentional artifacts and should not be simplified away if they affect codegen.
Object list updates are part of asm-to-C completion: remove the asm object from TEXT_O_FILES and add the new C object in the corresponding order.
Epilogue-unscheduled object lists also need to be updated when adding a new decompiled translation unit.
naming_conventions: The new helper name checkFighter2244 describes the Fighter x2244 resource cleanup behavior directly, using the existing field offset-style name rather than inventing a higher-level semantic name.
The split file was named ftclink_2.c, following the project convention used by neighboring fighter files such as ftdrmario_2.c.
Existing unresolved function names were retained as func_80149354, func_8014943C, func_8014945C, and func_8014947C because no stronger semantic names were evidenced.

Field names used in the matched code follow existing Fighter struct naming conventions: x2200_ftcmd_var1, x2244, xB0_pos, x2C_facing_direction, x21E4_callback_OnDeath2, and x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted 102 lines of ftclink.s after matching the remaining functions in C.
Used a separate source file for func_80149354 to avoid inlining func_80149268, per the PR note.
Declared unused stack locals such as int unused[4] in func_80149354 and int unused[2] in func_80149268 to influence stack layout/codegen.
Used two Fighter* locals, ft and ft2, both initialized from gobj->user_data, matching register/value reuse patterns from the assembly.
Stored func_802C8B28's return value in a temporary void* temp_r3 before assigning it to ft->x2244, preserving the compare/store flow visible in the removed asm.
Matched callback assignment by writing func_800EAF58 to both x21E4_callback_OnDeath2 and x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage when item/object creation succeeds.
Recreated cleanup behavior by calling func_802C8C34 on ft->x2244 and zeroing x2244 via checkFighter2244.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where a function was moved to a separate translation unit to prevent inlining.
Search for checkFighter2244 or x2244 cleanup reuse across ftclink and related item-spawning fighter code.
Compare ftclink_2.c file-splitting patterns with ftdrmario_2.c and other fighter _2.c files.
Search for PRs that corrected include/functions.h prototypes to enable matching newly decompiled fighter code.
